text.title: Behavior Parity Verification Worked Example
text.summary: One end-to-end example: a Python pricing service is being rewritten in Go. The team runs shadow parity across four ramp stages, catches an FX-rounding cluster at stage 3, fixes it, and signs off at stage 4.

section.title: Context
section.p: Scope: `/api/v2/quote` returns `{total_cents, line_items[], currency, tax_breakdown}`. Legacy: Python service `quote-py`. New: Go service `quote-go`. Both deployed; router controlled by feature flag `quote.shadow.go_pct`. Traffic: ~50k requests/day.

section.title: Step 1 — observable list (committed)
section.code:
Observable fields for /api/v2/quote: - response.total_cents (exact equality) - response.line_items[] (sorted by sku, then exact equality) - response.currency (exact equality) - response.tax_breakdown.country (exact equality) - response.tax_breakdown.amount (tolerance: ±1 cent)
Ignored: - response.request_id - response.computed_at_ms - response.server_version

section.title: Stage 3 (50%) report — freeze
section.note: diff_rate above 0.5% threshold, fx-rounding-eur-pt cluster open. Freeze the ramp; do not promote. Engineer investigates: Go code uses banker's rounding, Python used half-up. Fix lands in `quote-go`, re-run stage 3.

section.title: Stage 4 (100%) report — sign-off
section.note: Stage 4 closes with diff_rate well under threshold, FX cluster resolved. Legacy `quote-py` removal is scheduled for the next release window, not this one.
